# AI Evidence Synthesis
# https://evidencesynthesisai.com
# By Hopperlace Research (https://hopperlace.ai)

## About
AI Evidence Synthesis is an AI-powered systematic review screening tool with full auditability. It uses dual-run consensus screening where each study is screened twice by AI, and uncertain cases are flagged for human review rather than receiving forced decisions. The tool supports Level 1 (title/abstract) and Level 2 (full-text) screening with PICO-structured reasoning and a complete override audit trail.

## Key Innovation: Deference-Aware Evaluation
We propose a novel evaluation framework for human-in-the-loop AI systems. Standard evaluation penalises AI uncertainty — if the AI says "I'm not sure", it counts as wrong. In our deference-aware (DA) framework, appropriate uncertainty is counted as correct. The only failures are confident wrong answers.

This is formalised in: "Deference-Aware Evaluation for Human-in-the-Loop AI Systems: A Unified Quality Signal for AI Systems Operating Under Human Oversight" (Yuyu Shen, Hopperlace Research, March 2026). Available at: https://osf.io/a69yh/files/vj95h

## Three Evaluation Frameworks
1. Forced Binary (Framework 1): AI must decide INCLUDE or EXCLUDE. Traditional baseline.
2. Partial Evaluation (Framework 2): AI can say UNSURE but deferred cases are excluded from the denominator — hides workload.
3. Deference-Aware (Framework 3): UNSURE counts as correct. Only confident wrong answers are failures. Workload reported explicitly.

## Key Results (Donners et al. 2021, 258 studies, 9 LLM models)
- Claude Sonnet 4.6: 100% DA sensitivity, 0 H.Miss (zero missed included studies), 3 confident errors, 23.3% deferred
- Standard metrics rank Claude last (60% recall) because it defers — DA evaluation correctly ranks it first
- Open source pair (Llama 70B + DeepSeek v3): matches Claude on DA sensitivity and H.Miss at 1/15th cost, but with 17 errors vs 3
- Framework 2 produces 100% recall for Claude on the same data where Framework 1 shows 73.3% — the denominator hiding problem demonstrated empirically

## Models Evaluated
Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Anthropic), DeepSeek v3 (DeepSeek), Kimi k2 (Moonshot AI), Llama 3.3 70B (Meta), Mistral 3.1 24B (Mistral AI), Gemma 3 27B (Google), GPT-4o (OpenAI), Qwen 3 235B (Alibaba Cloud), DeepSeek R1 32B (DeepSeek)

## Dataset
SYNERGY dataset (CC0 licence): Donners et al. 2021 — Pharmacokinetics and Associated Efficacy of Emicizumab in Humans. 258 studies, 15 included, 243 excluded. PICO criteria verified from published paper (PMC8585815).

## Product Features
- Two-stage screening (Level 1 abstract, Level 2 full-text)
- Dual-run consensus screening
- Mixed-model consensus (pair complementary open source models)
- PICO-structured reasoning with per-criterion checks
- Immutable audit trail (original AI decision preserved, all overrides logged with who/when/from/to)
- Analytics dashboard
- Meta-miner for citation extraction from systematic reviews
- Free tier: 20 screenings/month
- Institutional access: contact hello@hopperlace.ai
